overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under CA Civil Code §1671
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may not owe liquidated damages on termination

overrides.override_summary: CA Bus. & Prof. Code §§20000-20043 provide termination/non-renewal rights that supersede FA
overrides.why_it_matters: California law may require good cause for termination and provide additional protections

overrides.override_summary: Virginia venue requirement may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisee may be able to litigate in California

overrides.override_summary: NY governing law provision may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: California law may apply instead of New York law

overrides.override_summary: Release does not waive rights under CA Franchise Investment Law or Franchise Relations Act
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees retain all statutory rights despite signing releases

overrides.override_summary: General release does not relieve franchisor from liability under Hawaii franchise law
overrides.why_it_matters: Hawaii statutory claims survive any release

overrides.override_summary: Termination/non-renewal rights affected by Illinois law (815 ILCS 705/19-20)
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois may require additional protections before termination

overrides.override_summary: Venue/governing law/jury waiver do not waive IL Franchise Disclosure Act rights
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees retain all IL Franchise Disclosure Act protections

overrides.override_summary: Any provision waiving compliance with IL Act is void; settlements and general releases are still permitted
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot waive IL franchise law protections

overrides.override_summary: MD law may supersede FA on termination and renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland may require additional protections and notice periods

overrides.override_summary: May sue in Maryland for MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law claims (3-year SOL)
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisee can bring claims in Maryland courts

overrides.override_summary: Release does not relieve franchisor from MD Franchise Law liability
overrides.why_it_matters: MD statutory claims survive releases

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire or acknowledgment waives claims under MD franchise law, including fraud in inducement
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot disclaim reliance on franchisor statements

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause; franchisee must receive 30-day cure opportunity
overrides.why_it_matters: MI law provides strong termination protections

overrides.override_summary: Must fairly compensate on non-renewal (if term <5 years); must renew on terms generally available to similar franchisees
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects against unfair non-renewal terms (though 22-year term exceeds 5-year threshold)

overrides.override_summary: Out-of-state arbitration or litigation prohibited
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees litigate in Michigan

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse transfer without good cause
overrides.why_it_matters: Transfer refusal limited to reasonable grounds

overrides.override_summary: 90-day termination notice with 60-day cure; 180-day non-renewal notice required
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer notice periods than FA's 10-30 day cure periods

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages prohibited (MN Rule 2860.4400J)
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees cannot be required to pay liquidated damages on termination

overrides.override_summary: Out-of-state litigation prohibited; jury trial waiver prohibited
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees keep right to MN court and jury trial

overrides.override_summary: Release does not relieve franchisor from MN Franchise Investment Law liability
overrides.why_it_matters: MN statutory claims survive releases

overrides.override_summary: NY Franchise Law governs NY Franchise Law causes of action
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law protections always available to NY franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchisees have broader termination rights

overrides.override_summary: General release in renewal/transfer may not be enforceable under NY law
overrides.why_it_matters: Releases at renewal/transfer potentially unenforceable

overrides.override_summary: ALL liquidated damages provisions deleted in their entirety
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees owe no liquidated damages whatsoever

overrides.override_summary: ND law supersedes; FA governed by ND law
overrides.why_it_matters: NY governing law replaced by ND law

overrides.override_summary: All out-of-state venue provisions deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: Litigation stays in ND

overrides.override_summary: Jury trial waiver deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees retain jury trial right

overrides.override_summary: Release does not relieve franchisor from ND Franchise Disclosure Act liability
overrides.why_it_matters: ND statutory claims survive releases

overrides.override_summary: Bankruptcy termination may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy as termination trigger may be voided

overrides.override_summary: Release does not apply to RI Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI statutory claims survive releases

overrides.override_summary: Out-of-state venue/governing law provisions void for RI Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees can litigate in RI under RI law

overrides.override_summary: Unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause per VA Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564
overrides.why_it_matters: VA requires reasonable cause for cancellation

overrides.override_summary: WA law (RCW 19.100.180) may supersede FA on termination/renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: WA provides additional termination/renewal protections

overrides.override_summary: Release waiver only effective post-negotiated settlement with independent counsel
overrides.why_it_matters: Pre-negotiation releases are ineffective in WA

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/litigation in WA for WA-purchased franchises
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees litigate in Washington

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void if employee earns <$100K/year or independent contractor earns <$250K/year; no-poach restrictions void
overrides.why_it_matters: WA limits noncompete enforceability and prohibits no-poach clauses

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees limited to reasonable estimated or actual costs
overrides.why_it_matters: WA caps transfer fees at cost recovery
